title: extension "under review"
author: svc-idee-bot
body:
we use ovsx cli to publish.

when extensions get stuck in "under review" it causes bugs for customers (they need several extensions that affect each other)

https://open-vsx.org/extension/salesforce/salesforcedx-vscode-services
Seems this has been happening for a while?  We've had several version that haven't made it to visible after publishing.

title: Use a prompts lib for better user input
author: gnugomez
body:
Right now the user input implementation is using `node:readline` which is a bit bare-bones. I would suggest to use something like [`@inquirer/prompts` ](https://github.com/sboudrias/inquirer.js/) for a better user-input handling.

This way we could have password inputs so when you paste the PAT in the terminal the actual PAT is treated as a password, hence not visible.

something like:
```
? Personal Access Token for namespace 'git-lens': *******************************
```

title: Migrating extension under different publisher
author: michprev
body:
Hey there,
is it possible to migrate an already existing extension under a different publisher (namespace), assuming I have claimed both the old and the new namespace?

I'm aware that it's possible to deprecate an existing extension and launch a new one as a recommended replacement, but these 2 points make me prefer a direct publisher migration:
- I would lose download counts
- VS Code marketplace forbids registering an extension with the same display name (i.e. I would have to rename), but allows a direct migration by contacting their support; I would like to migrate on both marketplaces in the same way, preferably without losing the current display name

title: Checking publishing permissions happens after initial validation with scanning enabled
author: netomi
body:
When scanning is enabled, some initial validations are performed before checking if the publisher has sufficient permissions to publish the extension.

This should be reversed as it is pointless to validate a package that cant be published anyways.

title: CLI should allow users to perform deleting actions
author: gnugomez
body: As pointed out by @svor in #1620, we currently don't provide an extension deletion flow outside of the webui, since `ovsx` bin is another way of using the application, thus we should support the extension deletion feature as well.

title: Relax checks for references when deleting an extension
author: netomi
body:
When deleting an extension, it is checked if this extension is referenced by bundles or declared as a dependency. If at least one occurrence has been found, the delete is rejected.

However there are cases where the delete still makes sense and should work imho.

Take this example:

Extension `Equinusocio.vsc-community-material-theme` has been deprecated for a while, but is still bundled from another extension `EdwinKofler.vscode-hyperupcall-pack-themes`, though not in the latest version of it.

```
Extension Equinusocio.vsc-community-material-theme is bundled by the following extension packs: 

EdwinKofler.vscode-hyperupcall-pack-themes-0.3.1, 
EdwinKofler.vscode-hyperupcall-pack-themes-0.4.2, 
EdwinKofler.vscode-hyperupcall-pack-themes-0.4.1, 
EdwinKofler.vscode-hyperupcall-pack-themes-0.6.0, 
EdwinKofler.vscode-hyperupcall-pack-themes-0.4.0, 
EdwinKofler.vscode-hyperupcall-pack-themes-0.3.0, 
EdwinKofler.vscode-hyperupcall-pack-themes-0.5.2, 
EdwinKofler.vscode-hyperupcall-pack-themes-0.5.1, 
EdwinKofler.vscode-hyperupcall-pack-themes-0.5.0
```

That makes it impossible to remove such a deprecated version as long as some extension version still exists that bundles it.

We should relax that constraint only for the latest version imho.

title: Stricter Namespace regulation
author: cstamas
body:
Currently, Namespace (NS) can be created by any user (who signed the publisher agreement). This is too volatile.

Instead, the process should be reversed: creation of NS should be administrative task, happening only after the "claim", proving NS ownership that has been accepted and vetted, and user should end up with "claimed" NS and be owner of it.
 
In short, non-admin user (or a user without some dedicated role) should not be able to create NS on his own, this should be administrative task instead.

A feature flag (configuration) should be able to control this behaviour, as on some (ie in-house) instances, this may be not needed, but on some (ie publicly reachable) instances may be required.

title: `ovsx` CLI tool needs to handle max-content-size before hitting the backend
author: gnugomez
body:
We introduced a new feature that exposes the `max-content-size` value on the `/api/version` endpoint #1937 together with a better messaging to guide the user.

This is the last step to make this setting fully configurable across all consumers.

title: Update handling of similarly named extensions
author: autumnfound
body:
As the Open VSX registry grows, malicious actors increasingly attempt to distribute malware through "typosquatting" and direct spoofing of popular extensions. To protect end-users and maintain platform trust, we are introducing strict validation mechanisms to prohibit duplicate extension names. This includes database-level constraints on unique identifiers and an intelligent pre-publication check that quarantines new extensions attempting to hijack the "Display Name" of highly popular, established tools.

Currently, we have a SimilarityCheckService built into the project that does fuzzy matching on all extension names and has a configurable distance for fuzziness that allows for tweaking of the sensitivity of the matching. This mechanism is part of the pre-publication checks that already exist and fulfil most of the requirements of the request to handle these maliciously named extensions.

Of the features remaining, we want to ensure that we have a way to easily distinguish the number of attempted spoofed extensions, a way to action them, and potentially an admin override of blocked terms. We will also want to ensure that the check is enabled and sufficiently strict.

_some issue details repurposed from internal request_

title: Open VSX webui needs a fresh look
author: gnugomez
body:
Open VSX has reached the incredible milestone of version 1.0.0. Now is an opportune time to consider its future and provide a new UX/UI design.

The current design appears dated, reflecting 2018’s aesthetics. Open VSX has evolved significantly with numerous features added, but <https://open-vsx.org> currently feels cramped and lacks sufficient space.

Several key issues I have identified:

-  The infinite scroll home page necessitates the use of a sticky footer, which is inconvenient and doesn’t provide adequate space for other information.
-  The existing UI lacks fundamental functionality as a global search tool #299 .
-  There’s limited space for additional information to assist the community in finding relevant information.
-  The overall UI design appears outdated.

My recommendations include:

-  Implementing a search bar similar to that found at <https://npmx.dev>.
-  Creating a non-infinite scroll home page with single-row sections, mirroring the structure of the official Microsoft Marketplace and allowing us to better utilise the home view.
-  Moving the current home search and list of extensions to a new /browse page.
-  Modifying global styles by rounding buttons and increasing padding to modernize the UI.
-  Move many of the menu items from the the nav bar, where they belong, to the footer.
-  Make the registry more keyboard-friendly

title: S3 `path-style-access` not honored in presigner
author: frenos
body:
When trying to setup a local deployment of OpenVSX against a minio instance I could not get it to work with `path-style-access`. Digging at the code this might be the problem:
-  s3Client is build with the option `.forcePathStyle(pathStyleAccess)` : https://github.com/eclipse-openvsx/openvsx/blob/d6690bba482290c821400cb40b9fe23be3985d5e/server/src/main/java/org/eclipse/openvsx/storage/AwsStorageService.java#L111
- s3Presigner does not have the option and so is always without pathStyle: https://github.com/eclipse-openvsx/openvsx/blob/d6690bba482290c821400cb40b9fe23be3985d5e/server/src/main/java/org/eclipse/openvsx/storage/AwsStorageService.java#L132

If I understand correctly the s3Client creates the correct url but then the presigner changes it.

Maybe someone with more insight could confirm my theory and create a fix.

Thank you very much.

title: Extension GitHub Tag Manager with Under Review
author: igormenin
body: Hello, my extension called [GitHub Tag Manager](https://open-vsx.org/extension/igormenin/github-tag-manager) is marked as Under Review in version 0.4.4, which was submitted. The last modification was to change the position of a modal on the screen to ask the user if they want to participate in Anonymous Telemetry, which has been implemented since version 0.4.0. The telemetry information sent is only to indicate if a functionality is being used; it only sends a HASH to identify the extension installation and reports the type of event that was used. The data is sent to PostHog.

title: Extension removed from the Registry and namespace[ci-ai-box]
author: shazhouguhong
body:
**Description:**
I recently noticed that my extension ci-ai-box.hll-ai-box and its associated namespace ci-ai-box have been removed from the platform. I have not received any prior notification or explanation regarding this action, and I am currently unaware of the specific reason behind the removal.
**Request:**

1. Investigation: Could you please help clarify why the extension and namespace were removed? (e.g., policy violation, technical issue, or security concern).
2. Restoration: If there are no critical violations, I would like to request the restoration of both the namespace and the extension.
3. Guidance: If there are specific parts of the extension or the naming convention that need to be modified to comply with platform standards, please let me know. I am more than willing to make the necessary changes to ensure compliance.

**Additional Information:**
**Extension ID:** ci-ai-box.hll-ai-box
**Namespace:** ci-ai-box
Thank you for your time and assistance in resolving this matter. I look forward to your guidance.Best regards,

title: `vira.vsc-vira-theme@2026.5.4` is published but inactive and not visible
author: equinusocio
body:
Hi Open VSX team,

We’re having an issue with our extension `vira.vsc-vira-theme`.

The latest visible version on Open VSX is `2026.5.3`, but version `2026.5.4` seems to have already been published and is now in an inactive state, so it is not visible on the registry page. We didn't get any clue o message about why is being set as inactive and how to solve it, also the latest version just fix some broken links in the README file.

When trying to publish it manually with `ovsx publish`, we get this error:

    ❌ Extension vira.vsc-vira-theme 2026.5.4 is already published, but currently isn't active and therefore not visible.
    See the documentation for more information:
    https://github.com/eclipse/openvsx/wiki/Publishing-Extensions


This is the log from the publishing action, there is no errors or messages about being disabled.

```text
Run HaaLeo/publish-vscode-extension@v2
  with:
    pat: ***
    dependencies: false
    extensionFile: vsc-vira-theme-2026.5.4.vsix
    registryUrl: https://open-vsx.org/
    packagePath: ./
    yarn: false
    dryRun: false
    noVerify: false
    skipDuplicate: false
Specifically, running GitHub Action HaaLeo/publish-vscode-extension@v2.0.0.
Package the Extension
  The extension was already packaged. Skip packaging.
Publish the Extension
  🚀  Published vira.vsc-vira-theme v2026.5.4
```

What we observed:
- Open VSX page only shows `2026.5.3`
- CI release workflow completed successfully
- Manual publish does not work because `2026.5.4` already exists, but is inactive

Extension details:
- Namespace: `vira`
- Extension: `vsc-vira-theme`
- Affected version: `2026.5.4`

Could you please check why version `2026.5.4` is inactive and either:
- reactivate it so it becomes visible, or
- remove it so we can republish it cleanly?


Thanks.

title: Add support to invalidate / purge CDN caches
author: netomi
body:
Open VSX does a lot of internal caching already, however when putting a CDN in front of the application the application needs a way to instruct the CDN provider to purge relevant caches i.e. when a new extension version is published.

This would allow to do even more aggressive caching, e.g. the `/vscode/gallery/<namespace>/<extension>/latest` endpoint

Right now, endpoints that potentially return dynamic data have explicit cache control headers `no-cache` to prevent that stale data is returned.

title: Request to regain ownership of namespace 'tongjinlv' after GitHub username change
author: jmpty
body:
### Description
Hello Open VSX Team,

I recently changed my GitHub username, which caused a system permission issue that blocked me from managing my original namespace. 

- **The Namespace I want to KEEP and USE:** `tongjinlv`
- **My New GitHub Username:** `jmpty`

### The Issue
I need to keep using `tongjinlv` as my publisher/namespace name because my extensions are already published under this name on other platforms (like VS Code Marketplace) and cannot be changed. 

However, after I renamed my GitHub account to `jmpty`, the system seems to have decoupled my new identity from my original namespace. When I try to publish updates to `tongjinlv` using my new token, the CLI throws an error: `❌ Insufficient access rights for publisher: tongjinlv`.

The system has locked the `tongjinlv` namespace to my old GitHub name, and I need to regain control of it.

### Request
Could you please manually fix this link in your database? I would like to transfer the ownership of the **`tongjinlv`** namespace to my current account (`jmpty`) so I can continue publishing under my original publisher name.

Thank you very much for your help!
